assessments.status_rationale: Today’s items focus on U.S.-Iran conflict dynamics and market reactions, but do not report any OFAC designation of an India-based company under an Iran-related sanctions program for Iranian crude/petroleum transactions.

Although multiple stories discuss the Strait of Hormuz and the Iran war, none of the provided bullets indicate a UKMTO/CMF/U.S. Navy incident update confirming physical damage to a commercial vessel from missile/drone/mine attack in the Strait or Persian Gulf.

No referenced Marsh, Aon, or WTW market update is included in today’s bullets, and there is no evidence here of a ≥20% YoY increase in U.S. data-center property insurance pricing tied to AI/GPU concentration.
Today’s news bullets do not include Broadcom earnings or investor materials, and provide no evidence of quarterly AI-related revenue reaching $6.0B.
No Samsung quarterly earnings (preliminary or final) are cited in today’s bullets; therefore there is no evidence regarding a ≥15 trillion KRW quarterly operating profit.
None of the provided items mention an OMB memorandum establishing government-wide post-quantum cryptography migration deadlines or milestones in 2028 or earlier.
While there is a DOJ-related political/civil-rights headline in today’s bullets, there is no reference to an unsealed indictment naming REvil and/or GandCrab leadership or core operators.
No Salton Sea-region DLE project announcement (FID or financial close) is mentioned in today’s bullets, and there is no evidence of a ≥20,000 tpy LCE commercial facility commitment.

The utility-related bullet is political (Phoenix activism) and not a utility 10-Q/10-K disclosure; there is no evidence here of a ≥25% YoY rise in residential bad-debt expense tied to reduced federal bill-assistance funding.

No JPMorgan 10-Q/earnings/supplemental disclosure appears in today’s bullets, and there is no evidence of a ≥30% quarter-over-quarter PCL jump explicitly referencing private-credit/private-market valuation/leveraged lending risks.
